content: Admin impersonation pitfalls: (1) Audit trails log the impersonated user's ID, not the admin — regulatory/SOC2 violation; (2) Better Auth plugin has no built-in audit log for impersonation events; (3) Admin-to-admin impersonation allowed if impersonate-admins permission is granted — massive privilege escalation risk; (4) Sessions last 1 hour by default with no auto-close on task completion; (5) checkRolePermission is client-side only — never use for server authorization; (6) Twitter 2020 and Microsoft Storm-0558 both exploited admin impersonation tools; (7) Support systems with impersonation logs become high-value targets — twice as likely to leak data; (8) impersonatedBy field in session exists but needs explicit audit logging wiring.
